title: Identify And Use Operator Station Display Controls To Select HMI Screens
summary: Use the documented operator station display controls to identify available HMI screen navigation methods, match function keys F1 through F6 to their named screens, and select screens using either the on-screen buttons, corresponding physical display buttons, or the Next Screen drop-down menu.
when_to_use: Use this runbook when an operator needs to identify the operator station display controls and use the documented navigation methods to select an HMI screen at the operator station.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to infer undocumented meanings or uses for controls beyond the mappings and behaviors stated in the source.
Do not use this runbook as a detailed operating guide for the individual destination screens, because the source packet only supports control mapping and navigation behavior.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Identify the documented display controls and use the supported navigation method to select the intended HMI screen.

safety_notes: Use only the documented control mappings and navigation behaviors from the source.
If a physical button does not navigate to the same screen as the corresponding on-screen button, stop and escalate.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the operator station display
Documented control mapping from Figure 4-18 and Table 4-4

access_required: Access to the operator station display
system_preconditions: Operator station HMI is available for viewing so the on-screen buttons, physical display buttons, or Next Screen drop-down menu can be used.

visual_references.description: Primary reference image showing the operator station display controls, right-side screen buttons, physical display buttons, arrow buttons, center select button, and Next Screen drop-down menu.
Example destination screen associated with F3 / Visu_MCP_Dual.
Example destination screen associated with F5 / Visu_Data.
Example destination screen associated with F1 / Visu_ManControl.
Example destination screen associated with F4 / Visu_IO_Diag.

steps.title: Locate the operator station display controls
steps.purpose: Identify the documented control locations used for HMI screen navigation and selection.
steps.instruction: Locate the operator station display controls on the right side of the screen and on the right side of the physical display. Identify the on-screen right-side buttons, the corresponding physical display buttons, the arrow navigation buttons, the center select button, and the Next Screen drop-down menu.
steps.expected_result: The operator can visually identify the documented navigation and selection controls.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can distinguish the right-side on-screen buttons, matching physical display buttons, arrow buttons, center select button, and drop-down menu.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot identify the documented controls or the control layout does not match the source figure.

actions.label: Locate display controls
actions.exact_action: Locate the operator station display controls on the right side of the screen and physical display.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Right-side on-screen buttons, physical function buttons, arrow buttons, center select button, and Next Screen drop-down menu.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented controls cannot be located.
The physical display control arrangement does not match the documented figure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Screens can be navigated in three ways: the Next Screen drop-down menu, buttons on the right side of the screen, buttons on the right side of the physical display. The physical buttons will navigate to the same screen as the corresponding button on the screen.

steps.title: Match function keys to the documented HMI screens
steps.purpose: Use the documented button-to-screen mapping to choose the correct screen destination.
steps.instruction: Identify the function key labels and compare them to the documented screen mapping: F1 = Visu_ManControl, F2 = Visu_MDs, F3 = Visu_MCP_Dual, F4 = Visu_IO_Diag, F5 = Visu_Data, and F6 = Visu_Alarm.
steps.expected_result: The operator knows which function key corresponds to the intended HMI screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The function key labels match the documented Table 4-4 mapping.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed function key labels do not match the documented mapping or the intended screen cannot be identified from the mapping.

actions.label: Verify function key mapping
actions.exact_action: Identify the function key labels and compare them to the documented screen mapping: F1="Visu_ManControl," F2="Visu_MDs," F3="Visu_MCP_Dual," F4="Visu_IO_Diag," F5="Visu_Data," and F6="Visu_Alarm."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Function key labels and the screen-navigation buttons used to access HMI screens.
Example destination screen for F1 / Visu_ManControl.
Example destination screen for F3 / Visu_MCP_Dual.
Example destination screen for F5 / Visu_Data.
Example destination screen for F4 / Visu_IO_Diag.

stop_or_escalate_if: The function key mapping does not match the documented source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 4-4 lists F1=Visu_ManControl, F2=Visu_MDs, F3=Visu_MCP_Dual, F4=Visu_IO_Diag, F5=Visu_Data, F6=Visu_Alarm.

steps.title: Use arrow buttons to navigate to an on-screen item
steps.purpose: Move the selection to the desired on-screen item when using the physical selection controls.
steps.instruction: If navigating by physical selection controls, use the arrow buttons to move to the desired on-screen item.
steps.expected_result: The desired on-screen item is highlighted or navigated to for selection.
steps.healthy_condition: Arrow button input moves the selection to the intended on-screen item.
steps.failure_condition: Arrow button input does not move the selection or the intended item cannot be navigated to.

actions.label: Navigate with arrows
actions.exact_action: Use the arrows to navigate to items on the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Arrow navigation buttons used to move to items on the screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Arrow buttons do not navigate to items on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Selection/navigation buttons: Use the arrows to navigate to items on the screen.

steps.title: Press the center button to select the navigated item
steps.purpose: Confirm selection of the currently navigated on-screen item.
steps.instruction: Press the center button to select the item currently navigated to on the screen.
steps.expected_result: The currently navigated on-screen item is selected.
steps.healthy_condition: Pressing the center button selects the intended item.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing the center button does not select the intended item.

actions.label: Select with center button
actions.exact_action: Press the center button to select the item navigated to on the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Center select button used after navigating with the arrows.

stop_or_escalate_if: The center button does not select the item navigated to on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press the center button to select the item navigated to on the screen.

steps.title: Use the Next Screen drop-down menu for direct screen selection
steps.purpose: Directly select the next HMI screen using the documented menu-based navigation method.
steps.instruction: If navigating by menu, use the Next Screen drop-down menu to directly select the next screen to view.
steps.expected_result: The selected HMI screen opens through the drop-down menu.
steps.healthy_condition: The Next Screen drop-down menu allows direct selection of the intended screen.
steps.failure_condition: The drop-down menu is unavailable or does not open the intended screen.

actions.label: Select screen from drop-down
actions.exact_action: Use the Next Screen drop-down menu to directly select the next screen to view.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Next Screen drop-down menu used for direct screen selection.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Next Screen drop-down menu is not available.
The selected screen does not open from the drop-down menu.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Screens can be navigated in three ways: the Next Screen drop-down menu, buttons on the right side of the screen, buttons on the right side of the physical display.

steps.title: Use either the on-screen button or corresponding physical button
steps.purpose: Select a screen using either supported button method while confirming the documented equivalence between the two.
steps.instruction: If navigating by buttons, use either the on-screen right-side button or the corresponding physical button, noting that both should navigate to the same screen.
steps.expected_result: The intended HMI screen opens whether the operator uses the on-screen button or the corresponding physical display button.
steps.healthy_condition: The physical button navigates to the same screen as the corresponding on-screen button.
steps.failure_condition: The physical button does not navigate to the same screen as the corresponding on-screen button.

actions.label: Use on-screen button
actions.exact_action: Use the button on the right side of the screen to navigate to the desired screen.

actions.label: Use corresponding physical button
actions.exact_action: Use the corresponding button on the right side of the physical display to navigate to the same screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Right-side on-screen buttons and corresponding physical display buttons used for screen navigation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical button does not navigate to the same screen as the corresponding on-screen button.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Screens can be navigated in three ways: the Next Screen drop-down menu, buttons on the right side of the screen, buttons on the right side of the physical display. The physical buttons will navigate to the same screen as the corresponding button on the screen.

success_criteria: The operator can identify the documented operator station display controls.
The operator can match function keys F1 through F6 to the documented HMI screen names.
The operator can select the intended HMI screen using the arrow and center buttons, the Next Screen drop-down menu, or the right-side on-screen or physical buttons as documented.
healthy_conditions: Function key labels match the documented Table 4-4 mapping.
Arrow buttons navigate to items on the screen.
The center button selects the currently navigated item.
The Next Screen drop-down menu allows direct screen selection.
Physical display buttons navigate to the same screens as the corresponding on-screen buttons.
failure_conditions: The documented controls cannot be identified on the operator station display.
Function key labels do not match the documented mapping.
Arrow navigation does not move to items on the screen.
The center button does not select the navigated item.
The Next Screen drop-down menu does not allow direct screen selection.
A physical display button does not navigate to the same screen as the corresponding on-screen button.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the physical button does not navigate to the same screen as the corresponding on-screen button.
Escalate if the control layout or function key mapping does not match the documented source.
Do not infer undocumented meanings or uses for controls beyond the mappings and behaviors stated in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary control layout for operator station display navigation and selection.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Screens can be navigated in three ways: the Next Screen drop-down menu, buttons on the right side of the screen, buttons on the right side of the physical display. The physical buttons will navigate to the same screen as the corresponding button on the screen.
Selection/navigation buttons: Use the arrows to navigate to items on the screen. Press the center button to select the item navigated to on the screen.
Table 4-4 lists F1=Visu_ManControl, F2=Visu_MDs, F3=Visu_MCP_Dual, F4=Visu_IO_Diag, F5=Visu_Data, F6=Visu_Alarm.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source packet does not specify whether production stop is required.
The source packet does not specify whether lockout/tagout is required.
The source packet does not provide detailed purposes or operating instructions for every destination screen named in the function key mapping.
The source packet does not provide a dedicated artifact for the Visu_MDs screen.
